name: PR-CV 232 El Rentonar Circular Trail
name_original: El Retonar - Montaña de Nieve (PR-CV 232) — Circular desde Torremanzanas
description: Hike the moderate 8.7-mile El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) circular trail from Torremanzanas, featuring panoramic views and a snow we
region_intro: Hiking around La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to traditional agricultural areas. The region features peaks like Els Plans Summit and Cerro del Racó de Xomarra, providing panoramic views. The Río de la Torre flows through the area, contributing to a varied environment. Terraced fields with almond and olive cultivation are also characteristic of the scenery. Best hiking trails around La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas The most popular hiking route is El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) — Circular from Torremanzanas, a 8.7 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the mountainous landscape, passing historical snow wells. Another top favourite among local hikers is Carrasqueta Pass – Els Plans Summit loop from Serra de la Carrasqueta, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.6 km) path. This trail offers extensive views from Els Plans Summit, including the Sierra de Mariola. Local hikers also love the Picnic Area on the Vía Verde – Viaduct of the Seven Moons loop from Urbanización Estepar, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas is defined by mountainous peaks, riverine environments, and traditional agricultural terraces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,100 reviews. Nearly 6,000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas?
A: There are over 130 hiking trails in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy walks to more challenging ascents, offering diverse landscapes from mountainous terrain to traditional agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas?
A: Yes, La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas offers around 20 easy hiking routes. These trails are perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk, often exploring the picturesque agricultural terraces and riverine environments.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas?
A: Hikers in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas will encounter diverse landscapes, including mountainous terrain with peaks like Els Plans Summit and Cerro del Racó de Xomarra offering panoramic views. The Río de la Torre flows through the area, and you'll also see traditional agricultural terraces with almond and olive cultivation.

Q: Are there any circular walks in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868561133/el-retonar-snow-mountain-pr-cv-232-circular-from-torremanzanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) — Circular from Torremanzanas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.9 km trail exploring the mountainous landscape and historical snow wells.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on the trails?
A: The region is rich in historical points of interest. You can visit the ancient Almohad watchtower, Torre de Maçanes, which gives the town its name. Trails also lead to features like the old snow well, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4752696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Canyo Ice Well&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6443352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corral dels Matets&lt;/a&gt;, which offer insights into past agricultural practices.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Many trails provide extensive views, especially those ascending to higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14661392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – Els Plans Summit loop from Serra de la Carrasqueta&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking vistas from Els Plans Summit, including the Sierra de Mariola and even the distant city of Alcoy. You can also find stunning views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas?
A: The trails in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,100 reviews. Nearly 6,000 hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any family-friendly outdoor areas in La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas?
A: Yes, the Municipal Park, adjacent to the urban center, is ideal for families. It features paved riverbanks, shaded picnic areas under poplar trees, children's play areas, and covered barbecues, offering a relaxed outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find trails that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21937797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – Cabeç de Corbó loop from Serra de la Carrasqueta&lt;/a&gt; incorporate these features. You can also explore areas around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/619834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pass&lt;/a&gt; for more challenging experiences.

Q: Are there any trails that feature interesting natural formations like caves?
A: While not directly on every hiking trail, the area surrounding La Torre De Les Maçanes/Torremanzanas does have notable caves. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4675122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Gelada&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4093326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sarga Rock Art Cave&lt;/a&gt;, which are points of interest in the broader region.

Q: What is a good moderate hike to experience varied terrain?
A: For a moderate hike with varied terrain, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21942784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area on the Vía Verde – Viaduct of the Seven Moons loop from Urbanización Estepar&lt;/a&gt;. This 17.1 km trail typically takes around 4 hours 44 minutes and offers a diverse hiking experience.
tips: The views from the top of Els Plans at 1,325 meters are simply amazing. It is reached from the town of Torremanzanas by a signposted path in good condition and without too much technical difficulty.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) trail?
faqs.answer: The El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) is a circular hike that generally takes around 4 hours and 50 minutes to complete. Komoot estimates the duration at approximately 4 hours and 26 minutes.

faqs.question: Where does the El Retonar trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) is a circular trail that begins and ends in Torremanzanas, Alicante. You can typically find parking within the town itself, as it serves as the main access point for the route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the PR-CV 232 trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including paths, tracks, and some asphalt sections. You'll traverse typical Mediterranean inland landscapes with terraced fields, especially almond and cherry trees. Expect to walk along the ridge of the Sierra de la Carrasqueta, and descend through ravines like the Barranco del Comanador.

faqs.question: What are the main natural and historical features to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter traditional corrals, meander along the Torremanzanas river, and pass by the well-preserved 'pou de neu del Rentonar' (snow well), a historical structure used for storing snow. The trail also offers extraordinary panoramic views from the Sierra de la Carrasqueta ridge, potentially including peaks like Puig Campana, Aitana, Montcabrer, and Maigmó. Don't miss the Els Plans Summit for impressive vistas.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232)?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean inland landscape, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking this trail, offering milder temperatures. While the trail name includes 'Snow Mountain,' it's essential to check local conditions if planning a winter hike, as snow can occur at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs are not explicitly stated. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the El Retonar - Snow Mountain (PR-CV 232) trail. It is a public access route.

faqs.question: What should I know about the trail's signage?
faqs.answer: It's important to note that the PR-CV 232 trail's homologation status is 'conditioned' due to identified deficiencies in both horizontal (paint marks) and vertical (directional posts and information panels) signage. Hikers should exercise extreme caution and pay close attention to following the route, especially while adaptation works are underway. It's highly recommended to use a reliable GPS device or a downloaded map.

faqs.question: Is the PR-CV 232 trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The PR-CV 232 is generally considered to have a moderate to medium physical difficulty, especially for those accustomed to mountain hiking. While not technically difficult, it includes short but intense climbs and some fairly vertical descents. Beginners with good fitness might manage, but should be prepared for the elevation changes and the need for careful navigation due to signage issues.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead in Torremanzanas?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail starts and ends in Torremanzanas, you can find basic amenities in the town. These may include cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options, making it a convenient base for your hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and Camí de la Carrasqueta.
